message:
fix(transfer): apply effective excludes to retrieval rsync before directory include

The retrieve and download commands build rsync argument lists with
--include="*/" (include all directories for traversal) followed by
--exclude="*" (exclude everything not explicitly included). The
effective excludes from .rchignore and .rch/config.toml were only
applied during upload, meaning retrieval would walk directories on the
worker that should have been excluded — most visibly the .beads/recovery_*
trees that can grow to hundreds of thousands of files and cause rsync
"overflow in recv_file_entry" errors (exit code 22) even when the
actual Cargo command completed successfully.

Both build_retrieve_command and build_download_command now inject
get_effective_excludes() patterns before the --include="*/" argument,
mirroring the upload path. The ordering is critical: rsync processes
rules first-match-wins, so excludes must appear before the directory
include to prevent traversal into excluded subtrees.

New tests:
- test_build_retrieve_command_applies_effective_excludes_before_directory_include:
  writes a .rchignore with .beads/ and custom-cache/ patterns, builds
  a retrieve command, and asserts all exclude patterns appear before the
  --include="*/" argument in the rsync argument list.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
